title: Butter-basted ribeye
note: A 12-minute pan, 13 minutes of patience.

title: Cheesecake cups
note: Three ingredients. Six hours in the fridge.

title: Bacon chip cookies
note: Salty, sweet, structurally improbable.

title: Mini donuts
note: Egg-yolk dough. Dunk in browned butter.

title: Shepherd's pie, flaky butter crust
note: Worth the Sunday.

title: Cannoli
note: Crisp shells. Whipped mascarpone.

title: Cupcakes
note: More soufflé than cupcake. In a good way.

title: Chicken pot pies
note: Cream, thyme, pulled thigh.

title: Spring rolls
note: Egg-white wrappers. Crackling finish.

title: The breakfast stack
note: Eggs, sausage, a crown of cheese.

title: Slow carnitas
note: The oven does the work.

title: Sunday bone broth
note: Twelve hours. One pot. No notes.

title: Chorizo skillet eggs
note: One pan, runny yolks, paprika oil pooling at the rim.

title: Gruyère soufflé omelette
note: Fold once. Pull early. Don't overthink it.

title: Beef-fat hash browns
note: Rendered fat in place of oil. Crisp like a record.

title: Sausage and egg muffins
note: Make a dozen. Eat through Wednesday.

title: Crispy bacon waffles
note: Bacon fat in the batter. Worth the swap.

title: Reverse-seared ribeye
note: Slow oven, ripping pan. The cleanest crust we've made.

title: Roast chicken with butter
note: The first recipe everyone should master.

title: Lamb shoulder, low and slow
note: Eight hours in. No basting. Pulls apart with a fork.

title: Pan-seared duck breast
note: Score the skin. Cold pan. Don't peek.

title: Beef short ribs, red-wine braise
note: A Saturday recipe. Worth blocking the afternoon.

title: Crème brûlée
note: Six hours in the fridge. Six minutes under the torch.

title: Brown-butter ice cream
note: No-churn. Tastes like toasted cream.

title: Lemon curd
note: Bright, thick, on the verge of breaking. Don't break it.

title: Cheesecake brownies
note: Two batters, one tin. Marble ribbons of either.

title: Pork rind nachos
note: Crunch, melt, repeat. The rest is on you.

title: Beef jerky, kitchen-oven
note: Six hours at 175°F. A week of road snacks.

title: Cured egg yolks
note: Four days in salt. The umami bomb.

title: Buffalo chicken bites
note: Crispy thighs, sauce, blue cheese. The trinity.

title: Chicharrones, fresh
note: Pork belly skin, slowly rendered. The crackle is the point.
